figures.caption: Fig. 4: Computation of reachable sets for COPP2. (a) Backward propagation of the backward reachable set B′k. (b) Forward propagation of the bidirectional reachable set Bk.
Fig. 8: Representative normalized profiles for one instance. The upper bounds are normalized to ±1. (a) TOPP2. (b) GOPP2. (c) TOPP3. (d) GOPP3.
TABLE I: Comparison of different classes of OPP solvers.

Fig. 5: Computation of backward reachable sets for COPP3 based on projection. (a) The one-step exact reachable set Bek with sampled vertices. (b) The proposed extension-based method in Section V-B1. (c) The baseline in [15]. The proposed method provides a more accurate estimation of the residual area.

Fig. 6: Computation of the one-step exact backward reachable set Bek for COPP3 based on searching. (a) The geometric projection relation between Bek and B̂k. (b) Searching along a line. (c) Searching along a plane.

Fig. 9: Scalability of different methods to n-axis paths. (a) Computational time in TOPP problems. (b) Relative optimality gap in TOPP problems. (c) Computational time in GOPP problems. (d) Relative optimality gap in GOPP problems. In (b) and (d), the objective value of the SOCP methods is considered as the reference.

Fig. 10: Real-world experiments. (a) The robotic platform. (b) The normalized command and feedback signals in TOPP. (c) The normalized command and feedback signals in GOPP. Both TOPP and GOPP are solved by the proposed RDDP approach.
